title: Projects
text: This page focuses on two academic projects I have completed during my time at Cal Poly Pomona, as well as connects those projects to my real operations experience.

title: Projects
text: This page focuses on two academic projects I have completed during my time at Cal Poly Pomona, as well as connects those projects to my real operations experience.

title: Projects
text:
Project 1: Used Car Pricing Research

Project Context
This project, completed for my Marketing Research course, was aimed at understanding what factors influence the prices of used vehicles. Particularly, analyzing what impact mileage, vehicle age, make, and model have on used car listing prices.


Research Question
Research Question:
How do mileage, age, make, model, and brand reputation influence used car pricing?


Data and Method

Data source: CarMax, Carvana
Sample size: 60 vehicle listings
Main variables: Mileage, vehicle age, make, model
Analysis method: Regression
Purpose of analysis: Determining what factors influence the price of used vehicles, in order to help used car buyers evaluate whether they are being fairly charged.



Skills Demonstrated

Market research
Data collection
Regression analysis
Business interpretation
Written communication



What I Learned
This project taught me that pricing decisions are influenced by vehicle mileage as well as make. Mileage being directly measurable, while make carried a more perceptual variable weight. Brand reputation has a signficant influence on the percieved value of a vehicle.

title: Projects
text:
Project 2: Interview An Executive

Project Context
For this project, I interviewed a local executive/founder. The interview was centered around value chain analysis. The aim of this project was to learn how an organization level leader evaluates performance across departments and effectively manages the value chain of their company.


Concepts Applied

Value chain analysis
Competitive advantage
Business-level strategy
Operations strategy
Leadership decision-making
Customer value
Organizational structure
Performance management



Skills Demonstrated

Interview preparation
Professional communication
Connecting theory to practice
Leadership analysis
Operations thinking



What I Learned
From the interview, I learned that strategy is not just abstract planning. Real business strategy shows up in operations, people, customer service, and execution. This project helped you think more seriously about leadership and management.

title: Projects
text:
Professional Application: McDonald’s Operations Leadership

Connection to My Career Goals
My academic projects and work experience connect because both involve data analysis and leadership planning. In the future, I want to use the data analysis skills I’ve built and leadership practices I’ve learned to help organizations improve across their value chain and operational understanding.

title: Isaiah Kirkpatrick
text:
Professional Profile
I am a senior Business Administration student at Cal Poly Pomona, with a marketing emphasis. Professionally, I have been a Department Manager at McDonald’s for the past year. My role is essentially leading a department at the McDonald’s location I’m assigned to that is responsible for a specific set of controlable costs and operations. Through these paralell experiences, I have had the opportunity to understand business from both an operational and academic perspective. My goal is to pursue operations management.

title: Isaiah Kirkpatrick
text:
Career Objective
My career aim, following graduation, is to pursue a role in operations management at a unit level of scale. Having worked across multiple departments within a McDonald’s unit, I have had the opportunity to manage multiple aspects of a unit level P&L. Additionally, my academic experience has provided me with valuable structures and knowledge that I can use to lead operations across departments and teams.

title: Isaiah Kirkpatrick
text:
Education
California State Polytechnic University, Pomona
Bachelor of Business Administration
Emphasis: Marketing
Expected Completion: July, 2026

title: Isaiah Kirkpatrick
text:
Professional Experience
McDonald’s — Department Manager
Ontario, California

Led People Department, responsible for hiring, training, scheduling, and labor cost control for a McDonald’s location earning $6 million in yearly revenue. Saved $21,000 in labor cost and reduced turnover by 48% during my year of leadership in the department.
Led Quality Department, responsible for equipment, food safety, inventory, ordering, and food cost control for this McDonald’s location. During my time in the department I consistently met food cost targets and led the department though 90%+ scores on health department, EcoSure, and corporate visits.
Currently leading the service department, responsible for customer satisfaction, compliant resolution, promotional marketing, and service times for a store ranked in the top 5 for service in my organization.

title: Google Analytics
text:
Business Insight

The site had 88,573 total users and 1,302,922 total events during the last 28 days.
The strongest event categories were page_view and view_item_list.
The Home page had the highest visible page views.
Engagement was moderate, with 54 seconds average engagement time per active user and 0.51 engaged sessions per active user.

title: Used Car Pricing Research
text:
Research Purpose

Analyze factors connected to used car resale prices
Focus on mileage, age, brand, model, and reputation
Use data visualization to communicate pricing patterns

title: Used Car Pricing Research
text:
Research Question
How do mileage, vehicle age, brand, model, and brand reputation influence used car pricing?

title: Used Car Pricing Research
text:
Data and Method

Data type: sample used car listing data
Main variables: price, mileage, year, brand, model, reputation score
Analysis method: visual comparison and trend analysis
Purpose: identify visible pricing patterns

Business Takeaway
The strongest differentiator in price in the study was vehicle mileage. Buyers can use this information when determining whether they are being charged fairly. Sellers can use this information to consistently price vehicles.
